authors: Sherman Wong, Zhenting Qi, Zhaodong Wang, Nathan Hu, Samuel Lin, Jun Ge, Erwin Gao, Wenlin Chen, Yilun Du, Minlan Yu, Ying Zhang
title: Confucius Code Agent: Scalable Agent Scaffolding for Real-World Codebases
storage_format: Hierarchical working memory (Markdown file tree with metadata tags and visibility scopes: session, entry, runnable) + persistent cross-session notes (Markdown in a per-session directory, e.g. research/findings.md, solutions/bug_fix.md, including hindsight notes for failures) + context compression summaries (structured plan with goals, decisions, TODOs, generated by the Architect Agent)
llm_used: Claude 4 Sonnet, Claude 4.5 Sonnet, Claude 4.5 Opus, GPT-5.2
input_tokens_per_task: ~104k tokens (Run 1 without notes); ~93k tokens (Run 2 with notes)
memory_size_over_time: Working memory grows during a session and is periodically compressed by the Architect Agent; persistent notes accumulate across sessions
token_reduction: -11k tokens/task (104k→93k) with persistent notes from Run 1 applied in Run 2
main_result: CCA achieves 59.0% Resolve@1 on SWE-Bench-Pro with GPT-5.2, surpassing OpenAI (56.0%); with Claude 4.5 Sonnet it reaches 52.7% vs Live-SWE-Agent 45.8%. Persistent notes reduce turns from 64 to 61 and tokens from 104k to 93k, with a +1.4pp gain in Resolve Rate.
performance_vs_baseline: +3.0pp vs OpenAI (59.0% vs 56.0%) with GPT-5.2; +6.9pp vs Live-SWE-Agent (52.7% vs 45.8%) with Claude 4.5 Sonnet; +1.8pp vs OpenHands (74.6% vs 72.8%) on SWE-Bench-Verified
limitations: PyTorch-Bench is not directly comparable with SWE-Bench-Pro due to differences in execution environment; note evaluation limited to 151 instances; meta-agent co-developed with the evaluation benchmarks; SDK not fully open-source
validity_threats: SWE-Bench-Verified is sensitive to the model's thinking budget; comparison with proprietary systems (Anthropic, OpenAI) may involve uncontrolled infrastructure differences; meta-agent refined on the same benchmarks used for evaluation may lead to overfitting
